item.title: Bitcoin Selloff Leaves Half of All Supply Trading at a Loss
item.description: Bitcoin’s latest retreat has been so severe, it has pushed more than half its circulating supply into loss-making territory.

item.title: AI Firms’ Explosive Growth Improves the Math for Late-Stage Investors
item.description: Later investments are producing much higher returns than in the past

item.title: Cosco Widens Cargo Beachhead in Spain as Car Imports Rise
item.description: Cosco has been awarded a 50-year port concession in Tarragona on the east coast

item.title: Jevons Paradox Gains Traction in Debate Over AI Job Impact
item.description: An 1860s theory is revived to explain the effects of a new technology on the labor market.

item.title: Thailand, Malaysia Clash Over Seafood Trade Curbs
item.description: A trade dispute between Malaysia and Thailand is escalating, with the latter saying it may raise the issue at the World Trade Organization.

item.title: Ghana Economy Grows 6.4% as Reforms Offset Iran War Impact
item.description: Ghana’s economy expanded at a faster pace in the first quarter as companies benefited from lower inflation and interest rates, with reforms helping offset the impact from the war in Iran.

item.title: All the Ways Wall Street Gets Paid to Fund AI Boom
item.description: Enormous valuations are rewriting the IPO playbook.

item.title: Dubai’s DP World Meets Bond Investors to Allay Worries Over War
item.description: DP World Ltd., one of the world’s biggest container port operators, has been meeting with fixed-income investors to discuss its performance since the outbreak of the US-Iran war as the Dubai logistics giant mulls its options for repaying a bond due in a few months.

item.title: Bitcoin Finds a Bounce, Not a Bottom
item.description: The cryptocurrency’s modest rebound may prove short-lived as structural frailties are exposed.

item.title: BNP Sees US Tech IPOs Fueling Appetite for Deals in Europe
item.description: Mega IPOs by some of the largest privately held tech companies are likely to fuel appetite for stock sales in Europe’s tech industry as well, according to a top BNP Paribas SA investment banker.

item.title: BOJ’s Ueda Hospitalized, Expected to Miss June Policy Meet
item.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda has been hospitalized and is expected to miss next week’s policy meeting, according to the BOJ.

item.title: Carlyle Seeks Banks for India IPO of Healthcare RCM Provider
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. has invited pitches from investment banks to advise on a potential India initial public offering of its recently acquired healthcare billing service business, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oracle Earnings Pose Next Test for Suddenly Shaky AI Stock Rally
item.description: The rally in artificial intelligence-related stocks is suddenly on shaky footing, and Oracle Corp.’s earnings report after the close represents the group’s next test.

item.title: BizLink to Buy Blackstone’s Interplex in $850 Million Deal
item.description: Taiwan’s BizLink Holding Inc. has agreed to acquire Blackstone Inc.’s information and communications technology business Interplex Datacom.

item.title: Xinjiang Chief Touts Trade, Diplomacy as Hormuz Disruption Bites
item.description: China is pitching its far-western Xinjiang region as a commercial gateway to Central Asia and Europe, courting neighbors at a moment when overland trade routes are drawing renewed attention due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Slowing Economy Gives Turkey Cover to Hold Interest Rates Again
item.description: Turkey’s central bank is poised to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged in the face of a cooling economy and subdued foreign currency demand despite rising prices driven by the Iran war.

item.title: Cambridge Aerospace in Talks to Raise at $3.5 Billion Valuation
item.description: Cambridge Aerospace, a British defense technology startup developing interceptor systems to protect against drones and cruise missiles, is in discussions to raise approximately $300 million at a valuation of around $3.5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Suez Canal Gets Oil-Tanker Boost Amid Hormuz Strait Shutdown
item.description: The number of oil tankers crossing Egypt’s Suez Canal surged by almost a third in April and drove revenue to the highest since early 2024, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz spurred an alternative Red Sea energy route.

item.title: Subtle RBI Rule Tweak Opens Door to $50 Billion Inflow to India
item.description: A tweak to a single sentence within an arcane central bank circular has opened the door for billions of additional dollars to flow into India under a new incentive plan.

item.title: Ant International Considers Raising $1 Billion to Boost Growth
item.description: Ant Group Co.’s international business is considering raising about $1 billion to accelerate growth, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Here’s a Look at How India Could Further Boost Dollar Inflows
item.description: India is weighing further steps to attract foreign investment into the country, just days after rolling out a sweeping package aimed at boosting inflows and shoring up its external finances.

item.title: India Boosts LNG Buying to Meet Fertilizer, Power Requirements
item.description: India has ramped up purchases of liquefied natural gas from the spot market as importers rush to replace disrupted Middle Eastern supply and meet demand from fertilizer plants, power generators and households.

item.title: Turkey’s Army Pension Fund in Talks to Buy Stake in Tekfen
item.description: Turkey’s Armed Forces Pension Fund, known as Oyak, launched talks to acquire a stake in Tekfen Holding AS held by a company whose management was temporarily seized by the government.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Chief Touts Higher Bond Yields in Investor Pitch
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank governor signaled it would allow bond yields to rise and left the door open for future interest-rate hikes during a roughly 90-minute call late Tuesday night with investors, seeking their return to Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

item.title: US, Iran Attack Each Other Over Apache Downed Near Hormuz Strait
item.description: The US and Iran exchanged strikes overnight after President Donald Trump retaliated against Tehran for shooting down an American Apache helicopter.

item.title: WH Smith Cuts Outlook, Seeks Capital as Iran War Weighs on Sales
item.description: WH Smith Plc lowered its profit outlook and set out a plan to raise capital as the fallout from the conflict in the Middle East weighs on sales at the retailer’s network of airport stores.

item.title: India Stock Fund Flows Drop Most in Three Years as War Drags On
item.description: Monthly inflows into Indian equity mutual funds shrunk by the most in three years as investors cut stock exposure amid concerns about the prolonged US-Iran war’s impact on the energy-import dependent economy.

item.title: Apollo’s Kleinman Sees Private Equity Capitulating on Values
item.description: Private equity “lost its way a little bit” during the easy-money era and firms will “have to start capitulating for sure on valuations” after borrowing costs normalized, warned Scott Kleinman, co-president of Apollo Global Management Inc.

item.title: Logitech Debuts $80 Travel Mouse With Unique Folding Design
item.description: Computer accessory maker Logitech International SA announced a sleek wireless travel mouse aimed at professionals and Gen Z, with a unique folding design meant to fit in a pocket.

item.title: Governments Are Selling Bonds at Record Pace as Spending Soars
item.description: Governments are borrowing from syndicated bond markets at a record clip as public spending surges.
